code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test02-JUL-2025Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or acute kidney injury. Hemoglobin A1c is low, which may reflect anemia or recent hypoglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying metabolic or renal pathology.
